verdict_reasoning: HNRNPK is a canonical heterogeneous nuclear ribonucleoprotein (hnRNP) family member, explicitly localized to the nucleoplasm. Its function is nuclear pre-mRNA processing and mRNA metabolism. While some hnRNPs shuttle transiently to the cytoplasm, none anchor to the plasma membrane or present surface-accessible domains. No membrane isoform, GPI anchor, secreted form, or clinical surface-targeting program is documented. Not a clinically engaged MHC-presented antigen.
